title: Log Retention Index Frozen Bucket Age Compliance

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: `rest` search against Splunk cluster manager or monitoring console
spl:
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| where match(title,"^(pci|cde|payment)_")
| eval retain_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,2)
| where retain_days<395
| table title, retain_days, totalEventCount, disabled
| sort retain_days
value: Reports oldest searchable bucket age for indexes storing PCI audit evidence so at least twelve months of immediately available history exists before cold or frozen archives.
implementation: (1) Adjust `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` to ≥395 days per policy (allow leap buffer). (2) Document frozen/archive retrieval for older periods. (3) Alert if `disabled=1` on PCI indexes. (4) Quarterly compliance sign-off screenshot.
visualization: Table (index, retain_days), gauge (min retain_days), single value (indexes under policy).
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands — correlate with change management windows.
